summary: The United States conducted new strikes on Iranian military infrastructure including missile storage sites, radar installations, air defense systems, drone facilities, and naval mining capabilities in response to an Iranian drone attack on a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz. Israel and Lebanon signed a framework agreement under which the IDF will remain in Lebanese territory as long as a Hezbollah threat persists, with a pilot program beginning in two areas for Lebanese Army deployment. Iran launched missile and drone attacks targeting Kuwait and Bahrain, with both Gulf states activating air raid sirens and air defense systems.
key_points: The US struck Iranian military surveillance and communication infrastructure, air defense sites, drone storage facilities, and naval mining capabilities in response to an Iranian drone attack on a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @yediotnews25/131988)

Israel and Lebanon signed a framework agreement under which the IDF will remain in Lebanese territory as long as a Hezbollah threat persists, with a pilot beginning in two areas where the Lebanese Army will deploy. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93273)
Iran launched missile and drone attacks on Kuwait and Bahrain, with both Gulf states activating air raid sirens and air defense systems to intercept the incoming threats. (Source: @VahidOnline/76719)

The IDF and Shin Bet killed Waleed al-Nehna, deputy commander of a Nukhba squad who commanded the October 7 infiltration into Israeli territory and was involved in directing kidnappings during the attack. (Source: @New_security8200/202116)

US CENTCOM confirmed strikes on Iranian military sites including surveillance systems, communications, air defenses, and drone storage facilities in response to a drone attack on an oil tanker near the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80137)

Iran's IRGC fired two missiles at Bahrain — one was intercepted from the ground and one fell without causing damage — and also targeted a US military base in Bahrain where joint US-Bahraini forces intercepted 9 Iranian suicide drones. (Source: @rodast_omiddana/74455)

The IDF struck and killed Hezbollah operatives armed with RPGs near Israeli troops in the Nabatieh area of south Lebanon, and separately destroyed a Hezbollah rocket launcher that posed a threat to forces. (Source: @manniefabian/58691)

A security incident in the West Bank settlement of Beit Aryeh involved two masked suspects identified by a resident around 4:00 AM, prompting extensive sweeps by security forces who found footprints and a breach in the perimeter fence. (Source: @inon_yttach/9177)

sections.executive_summary: The United States conducted a new round of strikes on Iranian military infrastructure including missile storage sites, radar installations, air defense systems, drone facilities, and naval mining capabilities in response to an Iranian drone attack on a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz. Israel and Lebanon signed a framework agreement under which the IDF will remain in Lebanese territory as long as a Hezbollah threat persists, with a pilot program beginning in two areas for Lebanese Army deployment. Iran launched missile and drone attacks targeting Kuwait and Bahrain, with both Gulf states activating air raid sirens and air defense systems.

points: The United States struck Iranian military infrastructure including surveillance and communication systems, air defense sites, drone storage facilities, missile storage sites, and naval mining capabilities in response to an Iranian drone attack on a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @yediotnews25/131988)

US CENTCOM confirmed strikes on Iranian military sites including surveillance systems, communications, air defenses, and drone storage facilities in response to a drone attack on an oil tanker near the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80137)

Iran's IRGC fired two missiles at Bahrain — one was intercepted from the ground and one fell without causing damage — and also targeted a US military base in Bahrain where joint US-Bahraini forces intercepted 9 Iranian suicide drones with no casualties or damage reported. (Source: @rodast_omiddana/74455)
Iran launched missile and drone attacks on Kuwait, with the Kuwaiti army announcing its air defense systems were actively engaging hostile missile and drone attacks. (Source: @VahidOnline/76719)

63 members of Iran's Assembly of Experts issued a statement declaring that reopening the Strait of Hormuz would violate commitments and constitute a strategic error, citing continued Israeli attacks in Lebanon and the Lebanese army's failure to withdraw from southern areas. (Source: @VahidOnline/76713)

Iranian IRGC spokesman General Mohammadi stated that the enemy is deceitful and unreliable, and that any violation of the ceasefire will be met with a more severe response each time. (Source: @SharghDaily/168506)

Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian stated that the Iran-US agreement was approved by over 90% of the Supreme National Security Council members, including senior IRGC commanders, and that negotiations for its implementation have begun. (Source: @VahidOnline/76713)

The IDF and Shin Bet killed Waleed al-Nehna, deputy commander of a Nukhba squad in Hamas's military wing who commanded the October 7 infiltration into Israeli territory and was involved in directing kidnappings of Israeli civilians to Gaza. He was the nephew of former Hamas political bureau head Ismail Haniyeh. (Source: @New_security8200/202116)

The IDF struck and killed Hezbollah operatives armed with RPGs operating near Israeli troops in the Nabatieh area of south Lebanon, and separately destroyed a Hezbollah rocket launcher that posed a threat to forces. (Source: @manniefabian/58691)

Israel and Lebanon signed a framework agreement under which the IDF will remain in Lebanese territory as long as a Hezbollah threat persists, with a pilot program beginning in two areas where the Lebanese Army will deploy. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93273)

A security incident in the West Bank settlement of Beit Aryeh involved two masked suspects identified by a resident around 4:00 AM, prompting extensive sweeps by security forces who found footprints and a breach in the perimeter fence; the event concluded with no suspects found. (Source: @inon_yttach/9177)

Prime Minister Netanyahu stated that the signing of the Lebanon framework agreement represents a fundamental change, as Lebanon is telling Iran and Hezbollah for the first time that it wants peace with Israel. (Source: @almogboker78/17614)

The IDF killed Abd al-Rahman Muhammad Abd al-Karim Ziyada, a Nukhba squad commander in Hamas who infiltrated Israeli territory on October 7 and looted an Israeli military vehicle, in the Shati area last week. (Source: @inon_yttach/9188)

The IDF killed Abd al-Rahman Muhammad Abd al-Karim Ziyada, a Nukhba squad commander in Hamas who infiltrated Israeli territory on October 7 and looted an Israeli military vehicle, in the Shati area last week. (Source: @inon_yttach/9188)
The IDF also killed Kamal Muhammad Hamdan Najiar, head of the tunneling unit in Khan Younis, in the Shati area last week. (Source: @inon_yttach/9188)

A security incident in the West Bank settlement of Beit Aryeh involved two masked suspects identified by a resident around 4:00 AM, prompting extensive sweeps by security forces who found footprints and a breach in the perimeter fence; the event concluded with no suspects found. (Source: @inon_yttach/9177)

The head of the Beit Aryeh-Ofrim council stated that the incident highlights the urgent need for increased security budgets for settlements in Judea and Samaria, strengthening reserve classes, and expanding technological defense capabilities. (Source: @almogboker78/17605)

Israel and Lebanon signed a framework agreement under which the IDF will remain in Lebanese territory as long as a Hezbollah threat persists, with a pilot program beginning in two areas where the Lebanese Army will deploy. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93273)

The IDF struck and killed Hezbollah operatives armed with RPGs operating near Israeli troops in the Nabatieh area of south Lebanon, and separately destroyed a Hezbollah rocket launcher that posed a threat to forces. (Source: @manniefabian/58691)

63 members of Iran's Assembly of Experts cited continued Israeli attacks in Lebanon and the Lebanese army's failure to withdraw from southern areas as violations of the Iran-US understanding. (Source: @VahidOnline/76713)
Military aircraft were reported flying toward southern Lebanon, with warnings issued for maximum caution. (Source: @redlinkleb/28688)
Iraq's Counter-Terrorism Service conducted a military operation in the Yarmouk neighborhood of Baghdad. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373264)
Iranian Foreign Minister Araghji arrived in the Iraqi capital Baghdad. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80171)

Several reports and videos emerged of extensive gunfire in Baghdad's Green Zone, following earlier reports that Iraqi security forces were attempting to arrest several politicians on corruption charges. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4449)
Iran launched missile and drone attacks on Kuwait, with the Kuwaiti army announcing its air defense systems were actively engaging hostile missile and drone attacks. (Source: @VahidOnline/76719)

Iran's IRGC fired two missiles at Bahrain — one was intercepted from the ground and one fell without causing damage — and also targeted a US military base in Bahrain where joint US-Bahraini forces intercepted 9 Iranian suicide drones with no casualties or damage reported. (Source: @rodast_omiddana/74455)

Explosions were heard in Bahrain with air raid sirens sounding across the country, and the Bahraini Ministry of Interior issued a statement urging all residents and citizens to proceed to the nearest safe location. (Source: @presstv/195939)
The tanker struck by an Iranian drone in the Strait of Hormuz was en route from Qatar to the port of Fujairah in the UAE. (Source: @New_security8200/202119)

(Source: @yediotnews25/131988)

sections.threat_assessment: The operational environment is highly escalating across multiple theaters. The US-Iran confrontation in the Strait of Hormuz has intensified, with Iran directly targeting Gulf states Kuwait and Bahrain through missile and drone attacks, and a US military base in Bahrain being targeted. The Israel-Lebanon framework agreement represents a diplomatic development but does not immediately reduce the Hezbollah threat, as the IDF maintains its presence in south Lebanon and continues active strikes against Hezbollah operatives in Nabatieh. In the West Bank, the Beit Aryeh infiltration attempt demonstrates continued militant intent against Israeli settlements. The killing of senior Hamas Nukhba commanders Waleed al-Nehna and Abd al-Rahman Ziyada indicates sustained Israeli targeting of October 7 perpetrators.
sections.regional_response: Gulf states Kuwait and Bahrain have activated air defense systems in direct response to Iranian missile and drone attacks, with Bahrain issuing nationwide air raid sirens. The US-Bahraini joint forces successfully intercepted 9 suicide drones targeting a US military base in Bahrain. Iran's political establishment is divided over the US agreement, with 63 Assembly of Experts members opposing the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz while President Pezeshkian claims over 90% approval from the Supreme National Security Council. Iraq's Counter-Terrorism Service conducted operations in Baghdad's Yarmouk neighborhood amid reports of gunfire in the Green Zone related to political arrests, while Iranian Foreign Minister Araghji visited Baghdad.
sections.intelligence_notes: The US strikes on Iranian military infrastructure represent a significant escalation in the maritime domain, targeting not only offensive capabilities (missile storage, drone facilities, naval mining) but also defensive systems (air defense, radar, communications). The Iranian response directly targeting two Gulf states suggests a strategy of broadening pressure beyond the US-Israel axis. The Israel-Lebanon framework agreement's pilot program in two areas for Lebanese Army deployment will be a key indicator of whether Beirut can assert sovereignty in the south. The Beit Aryeh incident, while resolved without casualties, reveals continued militant infiltration attempts in the West Bank and has prompted local officials to demand increased security budgets. The killing of Waleed al-Nehna, nephew of Ismail Haniyeh, represents a high-value targeting of Hamas's Nukhba command structure.
